body.u: I am now sixty seven years old, when I look back over my life three aspects of my life seem the most important, first - I left school at seventeen to work as a costing clerk in a factory. It was a good job with plenty of scope for promotion. My tasks were interesting I met people from all walks of life, my work included typing shorthand filing and other office duties. To me my job was all I wanted as a career, and the people I met seamedseemed to enjoy their work and life. I learnt to drive and my car was the first thing I saved up for from my wages. When I had enough money to buy my car it mentmeant alota lot to me, it was the simblesymbol of the money which I had earned during my first two or three years of employment. The second most important thing in my life, was getting married. I carried on working for a couple of years after getting married, trying to save up enough money to put down on a mortgage for a house. This was the most important thing I had ever saved for, which we could bring children up in.

The house was small but modern, a typical town house was small but modern, a typical town house on a new estate, it was home. After working for the money for a house we decided to start a family. We had two children a boy and girl with two years difference between the two. Now they have left home, they both have good jobs and families. Now we wonder when we will become grandparents, to us this is very impertantimportant it will make us feel young again, just like when we had our own.

The third most important aspect of my life, was retireingretiring. We had saved up enough money during the years, to sell our town house and to buy a cottage in the country, (this is a dream most people have). Here we are now living it is small but plesentpleasant and we are both quite happy. We still have hobbies I enjoy doing part-time typing for a small lociallocal firm, and my husband enjoys golf and fishing. We still go on holidays abroad and to us they are very important. One day I hope to go on a world crusecruise, to see the sights that I have always wanted to see during my life, and to live out the rest of my days enjoying life and everything it has to offer.
